label: Seamglass Meridian Stillhouse
description: Seamglass Meridian Stillhouse is a compact residence built as a faceted wedge of vitrified tile set against the city’s fault-limned basin wall. Its interior walls are lined with grounded gutters and shallow lensing basins that collect electrostatic glass fallouts, turning the copper-white twilight into stable, banded illumination. A sealed sleep alcove and a narrow work niche are partitioned by refractive screens that preserve privacy without interrupting the calibrated light. The roof is stepped into a low observatory shelf, its mirrored coping aligned to the outer ring pylons to keep local time by the auroral girdle.
